title: Tottenham win race to sign West Ham’s Mateus Fernandes for club record £85m
description: Tottenham have beaten Manchester United to the signing of the Portuguese midfielder, who is due to have a medical before completion of the deal
content:
Tottenham are poised to break their transfer record after beating Manchester United to the signing of Mateus Fernandes for £85m.
The Portugal international is heading for a new challenge after West Ham’s relegation from the Premier League and will st... [1646 chars]

title: Brother of late TV star Caroline Flack dies
description: Inquest opened into death of Paul Flack, who was found unresponsive at his Norwich home earlier in June
content:
The brother of the late TV presenter Caroline Flack has died.
Paul Flack, 55, was found unresponsive at his home in Norwich on 21 June before later dying at the Norfolk and Norwich hospital, according to the Eastern Daily Press.

title: Patient tested for suspected Ebola virus at Glasgow hospital
description: It is understood a patient was admitted to the Queen Elizabeth University Hospital during the early hours of Tuesday.
content:
A patient is being tested for suspected Ebola virus at a Glasgow hospital.
It is understood they were admitted to the Queen Elizabeth University Hospital in the early hours of Tuesday.

title: ‘Tonnes of rubble’: 58,000 buildings estimated destroyed in Venezuela earthquakes
description: Preliminary analysis of satellite data suggests magnitude of natural disaster could dwarf official estimates
content:
More than 58,000 buildings may have been damaged and destroyed by the twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela last week, according to a preliminary analysis of satellite data that suggests the scale of the destruction could dwarf official estimates.

title: LeBron James to leave LA Lakers after eight years amid Golden State Warriors link
description: LeBron James will continue his career with another team after leaving the LA Lakers - and is reported to be a target for the Golden State Warriors.
content:
NBA superstar LeBron James is leaving the Los Angeles Lakers and will continue his career with another team.
James, 41, has spent the past eight seasons with the Lakers and helped the franchise win the 2020 NBA title.
